message:
test(format): convert rich-table and theme tests from smoke-call to actual assertions

Seven tests under `src/format::rich::tests` and `src/format::theme::tests`
were calling the functions they aim to exercise and immediately
discarding the result with `let _ = ...`. Same pattern as the progress-
bar tests addressed in the earlier assertion-hardening batch (bead
p7xo / mock-code-finder scan): the tests passed whether the
underlying code worked or not.

src/format/rich.rs — assert on rendered output:
- `test_rich_issue_table`: render the table with `.render_plain(120)`
  and assert that both issue IDs (`test-1`, `test-2`), both titles
  (`First issue`, `Second issue`), and the type column (`task`) are
  present.
- `test_rich_issue_panel`: render with `.render_plain(80)` and assert
  the issue ID, title, description, type (`task`), and status (`open`)
  all appear.
- `test_format_status_badge`: verify both OPEN and BLOCKED badges
  render their plain text ("OPEN", "BLOCKED") and that the
  styled-span representation is non-empty.
- `test_format_count_badges`: assert the exact plain rendering of
  `format_count_badges(5, 2, 1, 3, &theme)` — "5 open 2 in progress
  1 blocked 3 closed" — and that the returned object splits into four
  spans (one per count).
- `test_build_completion_bar`: assert progress is 7/10 within
  epsilon, `!is_finished()`, and the 20-char plain rendering is
  non-empty.
- `test_rich_issue_table_without_type`: render with `.show_type(false)`,
  assert the issue ID / title appear and that the literal "Type"
  header does not.
- `test_rich_issue_table_max_title`: render with
  `.max_title_width(20)`, assert the truncated form
  "A very long title..." appears and the full pre-truncation string
  does not.

src/format/theme.rs — assert styles resolve to the right fields:
- `test_default_theme`: for each `Status` variant, assert
  `theme.status_style(...)` returns the corresponding field reference
  (Open→status_open, InProgress→status_in_progress, Blocked, Deferred,
  Closed). Additionally check that an unknown `Status::Custom("waiting")`
  falls back to `theme.muted`.
- `test_priority_styles`: CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW each map to the
  matching `theme.priority_*` field. An out-of-range `Priority(99)`
  falls back to `theme.muted`.
- `test_type_styles`: Bug/Feature/Task/Epic/Docs/Chore each map to
  `theme.type_*`. `IssueType::Custom("ops")` falls back to
  `theme.type_task` (documented behaviour: unknowns render as tasks).
- `test_dark_theme`: assert the dark theme resolves `Status::Open` to
  its own `status_open` style AND that this style is *different* from
  the default theme's — previously the test just verified construction
  didn't panic.
- `test_minimal_theme`: assert the minimal theme uses `Style::new()`
  (plain) for Open, `.bold()` for Blocked and Bug, and `.dim()` for
  Closed and Chore. Locks in the minimal theme's intentional
  ANSI-free contract.

No production code changes; only tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
